text:
Once I saw that, I could not ignore it.

Not because I am particularly noble. But because I had lived on both sides of it. I knew what learning felt like when the feedback was real and immediate and safe. And I knew what it felt like when it was not.

And the difference was not small. The difference was the difference between moving with conviction and moving with fear.

Mastery comes from doing. From failing and trying again, fast, in a space that can hold the failure. That is not a philosophy. That is just how humans learn things. It is how Caleb learned. It is how I learned to draw. It is how pilots learn to land.

Medicine had decided it was exempt from that rule.

It is not.
